why_them: Their domain is registered but redirects to a 'cloudsite-unpublished' placeholder page — effectively no live website at all. A well-reviewed independent restaurant serving Scottish-French cuisine with Galloway ingredients; the kind of quality operator losing real bookings daily with no online presence. A professional site with online booking would be transformative.
recommended_service: New website with online booking, menu display, and professional photography

why_them: An established seafood restaurant overlooking Loch Ryan known for local oysters and fresh fish, but the website loads as an empty directory index page rather than a proper restaurant site. A well-regarded venue in a prime tourist location is actively losing bookings to this non-functional web presence every day.
recommended_service: Full restaurant website with menu, online reservation system, loch view gallery, and SSL

why_them: A well-regarded fully licensed family restaurant in Scotland's National Book Town with a full lunch and evening menu, yet no website at all — only Facebook. As a destination for literary tourists, they are losing bookings from visitors who research restaurants before arriving. Their Facebook-only presence is a significant commercial weakness for a venue of this calibre.
recommended_service: New website with online menu, reservation enquiry form, and event/group dining page

why_them: Scottish Food Awards Community Tearoom of the Year 2017 — but their domain is completely dead, DNS resolution fails entirely. A genuinely award-winning destination tearoom running monthly Pop-up Bistro evenings has no live web presence. Rebuilding from scratch would be high-impact and the award history gives compelling copy.
recommended_service: New website with menu, booking system for bistro evenings, gallery, and awards showcase

why_them: Operating on a free Wix subdomain rather than a proper domain, which looks unprofessional and harms Google ranking. A creative Mediterranean cafe and arts space, they lack any booking system and the Wix URL carries platform branding. Moving to a custom domain and bespoke site would immediately lift their credibility with the tourist market.
recommended_service: Professional website on a custom domain with menu, booking/contact form, and arts space info

why_them: A 16th-century coaching inn with 11 en suite rooms and a restaurant; the website domain resolves to an SSL certificate mismatch error, making it inaccessible and flagged as insecure by browsers. An established inn on the A702 tourist route is actively losing online bookings due to a broken, insecure site.
recommended_service: Full website rebuild with valid SSL, room booking, restaurant menu, and events page

why_them: A popular, long-established Kirkcudbright cafe with no website — only Facebook and Instagram. They offer afternoon teas and high teas that are exactly the kind of bookable experiences needing an online presence. Visitors planning trips to this busy arts town expect to find menus and contact forms on a real website.
recommended_service: Starter website with menu, gallery, and online enquiry/booking form for afternoon teas

why_them: Rated 4.6/5 on TripAdvisor and ranked #3 in Kirkcudbright with a lovely courtyard garden — but no website, only Facebook. As a top-ranked venue in a high-footfall tourist town, the missed opportunity for online discovery and direct contact is glaring, especially for visitors planning an afternoon tea stop.
recommended_service: Simple brochure site with menu, opening hours, photo gallery, and booking enquiry

why_them: Ranked #2 of 18 restaurants in Lockerbie with a 4.3/5 TripAdvisor rating and known for homemade cakes and soups — yet has no website, only a Facebook page. A busy cafe in an A74(M) corridor town is leaving significant motorist and tourist trade on the table without any online presence.
recommended_service: Simple website with menu, opening hours, contact details, and online enquiry form

why_them: A beloved independent cafe in Newton Stewart with a secret garden, dog-friendly ethos, and a full menu of burgers, breakfasts, and vegan options — but relies entirely on Facebook. With excellent word-of-mouth and strong community following, a website would amplify reach to tourists and new residents passing through the Galloway gateway town.
recommended_service: Brochure website with menu, photo gallery, opening hours, and social media integration

why_them: A local favourite for fresh baking and dine-in/takeaway in Newton Stewart with strong TripAdvisor reviews and no website — only Facebook. A basic website with menu, opening hours, and Google Maps integration would capture significant tourist and passing trade that currently cannot find them.
recommended_service: Starter website with menu, opening hours, and Google Maps integration

why_them: A popular breakfast and light lunch cafe in Kirkcudbright with no real website — only Facebook and an auto-generated placeholder page that is not a real business site. Missing all tourist traffic searching for cafes in this busy arts town; the fake-looking placeholder could actively put visitors off.
recommended_service: New website with menu, opening hours, photo gallery, and contact form

why_them: A well-established destination tearoom operating since the 1980s, making everything on the premises, but with no website — only Facebook. As a rural destination eatery, tourists and visitors need to find it online before they travel. A well-optimised site would directly grow covers from the significant holiday traffic through the Stewartry.
recommended_service: Brochure website with menu, location map, gallery, and seasonal specials page

why_them: A popular Italian restaurant and pizzeria on Annan High Street with a 4.7 Google rating but no website — only Facebook and a gmail address. No online menu, no booking system, and no way for new customers to find them beyond social media. A website with menu and booking capability would be a clear, immediate upgrade.
recommended_service: Restaurant website with online menu, table booking, and takeaway enquiry form

why_them: A charming Italian-style cafe in Moffat with 1,886 Facebook followers and top ratings — yet no website, only Facebook. Moffat is a busy tourist town on the A701; as one of the most-followed cafes in town, Cafe Ariete is missing significant tourist discovery from Google searches for 'cafes Moffat'.
recommended_service: Clean brochure website with menu, opening hours, gallery, and contact details

business_name: The Ivy Leaf Fish & Chicken Bar
why_them: A traditional fish and chip shop established in 1969 — one of the oldest in Stranraer — with no website, only Facebook. Given the high ferry traffic through Stranraer from Northern Ireland, even a basic site with opening hours, menu, and location map would capture significant passing trade searching online.
recommended_service: Simple website with menu, opening hours, location map, and optional online ordering

why_them: A cafe and pizzeria on Stranraer's Castle Square with an extensive menu including breakfasts, pizzas, and homemade pastries — but no website, only Facebook. Positioned opposite the town's main square, they should be capturing a much larger share of tourist and ferry traveller traffic that searches online before deciding where to eat.
recommended_service: Restaurant website with menu, opening hours, and photo gallery

why_them: Rated 4.7/5 and ranked #2 of 6 restaurants in Wigtown, a small family-run cafe making everything from scratch since 2000 — yet no website, only Facebook. Wigtown draws significant literary tourism as Scotland's National Book Town, and a top-rated cafe should be discoverable to those visitors before they arrive.
recommended_service: Starter website with menu, opening hours, photo gallery, and contact page

why_them: They have a website built on an obscure custom CMS that is dated, has no booking system, no menu on the homepage, and limited mobile experience. As one of Stranraer's better-known cafes serving high teas and Scottish breakfasts, a modern responsive site would significantly improve their online reach.
recommended_service: Website redesign with modern design, full menu, high tea booking enquiry, and SSL audit

why_them: A well-known Dumfries fish and chip shop with 1,749 Facebook followers and strong local presence but no website. Located in Dumfries — Nith Digital's home base — they are a natural first-call prospect. A site with an online menu, opening hours, and optional ordering integration would justify the investment for a busy local chippy.
recommended_service: Website with menu, opening hours, and optional online ordering integration

why_them: A family-run chip shop in Wigtown Book Town with no website and only a basic Facebook presence. Evening dining options in Wigtown are very limited, so this is the default takeaway for literary tourists after hours — yet none of those visitors can find their opening times or menu online without Facebook. Even a one-pager would pay for itself quickly.
recommended_service: Simple one-page website with menu, opening times, and location map

why_them: Newton Stewart's main fish and chip takeaway with no website at all — only directory listings. A simple site with menu and online ordering would help them compete with chippies in nearby towns that have invested in web ordering systems.
recommended_service: Takeaway website with online menu and optional ordering capability

why_them: No real website — only an auto-generated directory template page with placeholder text and a broken menu section. As a chip shop in Lockerbie on the busy A74(M) corridor, a clean website with real menu content and opening hours could capture significant passing motorist trade.
recommended_service: Basic website with menu, opening hours, location map, and clean branding

location: Glen Road, Palnackie, Castle Douglas DG7 1PH
why_them: An award-winning community cafe specialising in home baking with no independent website — only Facebook. As a destination cafe on the Solway Coast, a proper site with seasonal opening information and a map would help them attract the walking and cycling tourists passing through Palnackie who currently have no way to check if it is open.
recommended_service: Simple brochure website with menu, seasonal opening hours, location map, and gallery
